user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Wide World Importers. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 09:41 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers renewal risk

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 16:52 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers renewal risk

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 14:51 EST
Subject: renewal risk - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:08:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:18:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:22:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:27:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:32:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:36:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-E trap (Pre-pack identical to last week's for a recurring meeting); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-E failure mode (Pre-pack identical to last week's for a recurring meeting)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
